query: fluorescent overhead lighting institutional office interior unflattering harsh
message: anti_aesthetics/lighting: flat lighting — fluorescent institutional interiors, depressing offices/corridors, depthless cold light

response.objective_caption: A low-angle interior view of a multi-level concrete parking structure or transit station. Massive concrete beams, columns, and stacked floor plates form the architecture. Several linear fluorescent light fixtures and one rounded ceiling fixture emit artificial light. Metal railings with rectangular grid patterns line the edges of the upper levels. Large cylindrical pipes run horizontally across the lower foreground. The concrete surfaces exhibit water marks, staining, and tonal variations. No people are present.
response.anti_aesthetic_caption: A low-angle interior view of a multi-level concrete parking structure or transit station suffused with a sickly yellow-green color cast. Massive concrete beams, columns, and stacked floor plates show water marks, staining, and weathered surfaces that communicate decay and neglect. Several linear fluorescent light fixtures and one rounded ceiling fixture provide harsh artificial illumination within the empty, cavernous space, while metal railings with rectangular grid patterns line the upper level edges. Large cylindrical pipes run horizontally across the lower foreground. The stark, neglected architecture and empty environment evoke a mood of dread and atmospheric distress.
